title: Problem with creating containers with --user permissions in linux (fedora)
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue

Disregard this issue, due to I'm in doubt whether it is the expected behavior with Docker:

Note, that I've an function alias for podman as docker:
```
set -a # export the following functions as alias'
docker() { GID=$(id -g); podman "$@"; }
set +a # stop exporting
```

When I try to start a container with user permissions, podman either creates the container, but the container exits with an error or doesn't create the container, depending on the use cases whether --mount or --volume (-v) options are used:

--mount option used:
```
sad@fedora src]$ docker run -it -d --user "$(id -u):$(id -g)" --mount type=bind,source=$HOME/projects,target=/projects crops/poky:ubuntu-22.04
09ad62c1caa234f6ebe9d1706380aab1a5c38ce394f06304afb169be47abf7f1
[sad@fedora src]$ docker ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                              COMMAND     CREATED             STATUS                         PORTS       NAMES
09ad62c1caa2  docker.io/crops/poky:ubuntu-22.04              About a minute ago  Exited (1) About a minute ago              compassionate_driscoll
[sad@fedora src]$ docker container prune -f
09ad62c1caa234f6ebe9d1706380aab1a5c38ce394f06304afb169be47abf7f1
```

--volume (-v) option used:
```
[sad@fedora src]$ docker run --rm -it -d --user $(id -u):$(id -g) -v $HOME/projects:/projects:z crops/poky:ubuntu-22.04
b2ec67c163f9aae8a44ff9dd4a57547f266923b4b6d6120887d58b24772246f7
[sad@fedora src]$ docker ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue


```
sad@fedora src]$ docker run -it -d --user "$(id -u):$(id -g)" --mount type=bind,source=$HOME/projects,target=/projects crops/poky:ubuntu-22.04
09ad62c1caa234f6ebe9d1706380aab1a5c38ce394f06304afb169be47abf7f1
[sad@fedora src]$ docker ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                              COMMAND     CREATED             STATUS                         PORTS       NAMES
09ad62c1caa2  docker.io/crops/poky:ubuntu-22.04              About a minute ago  Exited (1) About a minute ago              compassionate_driscoll
[sad@fedora src]$ docker container prune -f
09ad62c1caa234f6ebe9d1706380aab1a5c38ce394f06304afb169be47abf7f1
[sad@fedora src]$ docker ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
[sad@fedora src]$ docker run -it -d --user $(id -u):$(id -g) --mount type=bind,source=$HOME/projects,target=/projects crops/poky:ubuntu-22.04
29452a10aab36dfcdb0d0f9cacf3ea045bbf3c186d06238d648409fc6c4d733f
[sad@fedora src]$ docker ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                              COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS                    PORTS       NAMES
29452a10aab3  docker.io/crops/poky:ubuntu-22.04              3 seconds ago  Exited (1) 3 seconds ago              gracious_rhodes
[sad@fedora src]$ docker container prune -f
29452a10aab36dfcdb0d0f9cacf3ea045bbf3c186d06238d648409fc6c4d733f
[sad@fedora src]$ docker ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
[sad@fedora src]$ docker run --rm -it -d --user $(id -u):$(id -g) -v $HOME/projects:/projects:z crops/poky:ubuntu-22.04
b2ec67c163f9aae8a44ff9dd4a57547f266923b4b6d6120887d58b24772246f7
[sad@fedora src]$ docker ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
[sad@fedora src]$ 
```


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

In the --mount case, the container exited with an error and in the --volume (-v) case, it wasn't created at all, even though the behavior of podman/docker said so... 

Either the container should have been created or an error message should have been issued.

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

I'm in doubt whether I expected a running container with issued user permissions in both use cases or an error message.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

comments.body: I'm in doubt whether above behavior is expected with Docker...
